decisions.title: provision Kafka event backbone at 2 GB/s with 4-year growth headroom
decisions.rationale: 2 GB/s provisioning accepted as 10× margin over 200 MB/s measured peak, projecting adequacy through 2027. Core assumption of uniform load distribution refuted by PIR-2024-031: correlated enterprise telemetry bursts reached 2.1 GB/s within 14 months, saturating the cluster. Capacity increased 3× post-incident.
evidence: design: 2 GB/s = 10× margin over 200 MB/s peak; 30%/yr growth → adequate through 2027 (platform design spec, 2023-04-12)
Q4 2023 review: consumer lag grew from <1s to 8 min average; broker approaching configured limits — root cause unknown at time (capacity review, 2023-12-08)
PIR-2024-031: peak throughput 2.1 GB/s exceeded rated capacity; enterprise telemetry storms from overlapping business-hour time zones; 4-hour outage; 3× capacity increase applied (2024-06-20)

hypotheses.text: telemetry events distribute approximately uniformly across the business day, making 2 GB/s adequate through at least 2027
hypotheses.refuting_evidence: PIR-2024-031: enterprise customers exhibit bursty correlated load during overlapping business hours (NA + APAC); peak 2.1 GB/s exceeded 2 GB/s ceiling within 14 months of deployment
